question_state: The question asks for the highest daily DCOILBRENTEU value during May 13-15, 2026. FRED data for this window was not yet released at forecast time (last observation May 1 at $118.26, next release May 13). The forecast had to rely on ICE Brent futures settlements and news reports as proxies. Brent was at $107.77 on May 12 with strong Iran-driven momentum, but early signs of profit-taking on May 13 and EIA's $106 forecast capped the upside estimate.
factors_tracked: FRED DCOILBRENTEU data availability and lag
ICE Brent futures settlement vs DCOILBRENTEU spot price mapping
Iran-US geopolitical tension and supply risk premium
Short-term technical momentum (3-day rally) and profit-taking risk

evidence_surfaced.claim: ICE Brent front-month settled at $107.77 on May 12, 2026, up 3.42% with 3 consecutive sessions of gains ($7.71 rally over 3 days)
FRED DCOILBRENTEU last observation was $118.26 on May 1, 2026; next release scheduled May 13 — meaning May 13-15 data not yet available at forecast time
Iran-US ceasefire is 'on life support' per Trump, keeping supply risk premium elevated
EIA STEO forecasts Brent at ~$106/bbl for May-June 2026, below current market levels, suggesting prices may be overextended on geopolitical premium
Brent crude slipped after the three-day rally on May 13 as markets tracked Iran ceasefire developments and Trump-Xi summit
Robinhood prediction market priced 51¢ for Brent >$107.50 on May 13, implying roughly even odds of staying above that level

judgment_trajectory: Initial anchor: FRED DCOILBRENTEU showed $118.26 on May 1, but this was stale data from before a significant price drop
Discovered ICE Brent at $107.77 on May 12 via Morningstar — much lower than the stale FRED figure, indicating a sharp decline earlier in May
Iran-US tensions provided upward pressure, but EIA forecast of $106 suggested current levels already embed a geopolitical premium
May 13 news showed Brent slipping after the 3-day rally, capping upside
Final estimate: modest upside from $107.77 to ~$109.50, reflecting continued tension premium but limited by profit-taking risk and EIA guidance
what_worked: Morningstar/Dow Jones settlement report provided the most reliable and precise current price anchor ($107.77 on May 12)
CNBC and Times of India news articles gave real-time context on Iran tensions and profit-taking dynamics
Robinhood prediction market provided an independent probabilistic check on the price level
EIA STEO forecast served as a useful sanity check against over-extrapolation of short-term momentum
what_misled: The stale FRED DCOILBRENTEU value of $118.26 from May 1 initially suggested much higher prices than actually prevailed by May 12 — significant time was spent reconciling this discrepancy
Multiple attempts to access FRED CSV data and EIA API failed or returned only stale data, consuming tool budget without yielding fresh observations
FOREX24.PRO forecast site showed a live price of ~$111.20 which may have been inaccurate or from a different instrument, creating confusion about the true current level
unresolved_concerns: Exact mapping between ICE Brent futures settlement and DCOILBRENTEU spot price — could differ by $1-3
Whether FRED will publish May 13-15 data before or after the forecast window closes
Major escalation (Hormuz Strait closure, military strike) could spike prices well above $110; de-escalation could drop below $105
The $118.26 FRED figure from May 1 vs $107.77 ICE settlement on May 12 suggests a large gap — unclear if DCOILBRENTEU will converge toward ICE levels or retain a premium
